summary: Soundboard recording of decent quality captures a stellar Viola Lee Blues performance that dominates the fragmentary show.
review: The GD‑67 fragment is prized for its 20‑minute “Viola Lee Blues,” which reviewers describe as smoking, relentless and one of the best versions from ’67. The soundboard source is generally clear with only a faint hiss and an abrupt tape cut at the end, making it good enough for most listeners. Aside from the standout Viola Lee, the rest of the set is considered filler, with New Potato Caboose and Born Cross‑Eyed receiving lukewarm remarks.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating of 4.6 across 16 reviews, overwhelmingly positive comments about the Viola Lee Blues and generally acceptable recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mostly clear sound with a small amount of hiss; occasional tape cut‑off near the end of the track, but overall crisp enough for listening.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Only a couple of songs are present in this fragment; the flow is limited but the featured Viola Lee Blues provides a high‑energy centerpiece.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work on Viola Lee is repeatedly called “smoking,” “compelling,” and full of inventive licks; his banter also adds character.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass counterpoint is noted as a key part of the improvisation, even echoing the Caution jam motif during the track.
band_member_comments.Keys: The keyboards attempt a Caution‑style jam within Viola Lee but never fully commit; overall contribution described as “weird” and experimental.
